DANCE is a 4pi array of 160 BaF2 crystals
designed for studying neutron capture cross sections
on small samples. DANCE was located on flight path 14,

Incident neutron energy was determined by time
of flight. Width of the proton pulses was 125 ns and
Neutrons are generated at LANSCE via
spallation reactions when an 800 MeV proton beam
strikes a tungsten target. The resulting neutron flux
peaks near thermal energy and is approximately
proportional to 1/En over the range of measurements.
